/cwe/weakness/581.Name: Object Model Violation: Just One of Equals and Hashcode Defined
/cwe/weakness/581.Description: The product does not maintain equal hashcodes for equal objects.
/cwe/weakness/581.ExtendedDescription: Java objects are expected to obey a number of invariants related to equality. One of these invariants is that equal objects must have equal hashcodes. In other words, if a.equals(b) == true then a.hashCode() == b.hashCode().

CommonConsequences.Note: If this invariant is not upheld, it is likely to cause trouble if objects of this class are stored in a collection. If the objects of the class in question are used as a key in a Hashtable or if they are inserted into a Map or Set, it is critical that equal objects have equal hashcodes.

PotentialMitigations.Description: Both Equals() and Hashcode() should be defined.
